stimulus: Although those who flocked to the Klondike in 1897 imagined pulling nuggets from gravel bars with a simple pan, the region's richest gold lay frozen in permafrost layers far below the surface, well beyond the reach of solitary miners. ______blank by 1900, most had abandoned their independent claims and hired on with one of the few syndicates capable of running the dredges and hydraulic operations the terrain demanded.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "As a result" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that many miners abandoned independent claims and joined syndicates—is a consequence of the previous information about the inaccessibility of the Klondike's richest gold to solitary miners.

Choice A is incorrect because "furthermore" illogically signals that this information merely adds another point, when it is in fact a consequence of the inaccessibility described earlier. Choice B is incorrect because "still" illogically signals a contrast or exception, when this sentence presents a direct outcome of the previous information. Choice D is incorrect because "next" illogically signals merely the next step in a process, when this sentence describes a consequence rather than a chronological continuation.
